title: Giving an AI Agent Gmail Access: 19 Steps and Still No Success
summary: A developer attempted to grant an AI agent access to Gmail but encountered a cumbersome 19-step process that ultimately failed. This highlights the challenges of integrating AI with existing email systems.
body:
A developer recently attempted to give an AI agent access to their Gmail account, only to find the process incredibly complex and ultimately unsuccessful. The endeavor required 19 separate steps, including setting up OAuth, configuring API permissions, and troubleshooting various authentication issues. Despite following all the necessary procedures, the AI agent was unable to effectively interact with the email system.

This experience underscores the significant hurdles in integrating AI with existing email platforms. While AI agents promise to streamline workflows, the current infrastructure is not designed to seamlessly accommodate them. The complexity of the process suggests that developers need more user-friendly tools and APIs to facilitate such integrations.

Moving forward, the developer plans to continue exploring alternative solutions, such as building a custom email client tailored for AI agents. The broader AI community is also likely to push for better integration tools, as the demand for AI-assisted email management grows. However, until more straightforward solutions are available, users may continue to face similar challenges.
twitterThread:
🤖 Gmail + AI = 19 steps. And it STILL didn't work.

Here's the wild part:

A developer tried giving an AI agent access to Gmail. Took 19 steps. Failed.

Let that sink in.

The process involved OAuth, API permissions, and endless troubleshooting. Still, no success.

This is the state of AI + email integration today.
Here's why this matters: AI agents could revolutionize email management. But the current infrastructure is holding them back.

The future of AI-assisted email depends on better tools. Until then, we're stuck with clunky workarounds.
